creator: ports@thelanman.net
text:
Created attachment 193125
poudriere build log

I upgraded my poudriere jails to 11.1-P9 and now go is failing to build. It built successfully about a month ago when I was running P4.

Attached is the poudriere build log. If I run a "testport" it seems to segfault when doing a "make" in the port.

These builds are running on a AWS instance.

Right at the top of the build I get:


cd /wrkdirs/usr/ports/lang/go/work/go/src &&  GOROOT=/wrkdirs/usr/ports/lang/go/work/go GOROOT_FINAL=/usr/local/g     o  GOROOT_BOOTSTRAP=/usr/local/go14  GOBIN= GOARCH=amd64 GOOS=freebsd  GO386=  /bin/sh make.bash
-ap: not found
go: not found
Building Go cmd/dist using /usr/local/go14.
Building Go toolchain1 using /usr/local/go14.
Building Go bootstrap cmd/go (go_bootstrap) using Go toolchain1.
fatal error: unexpected signal during runtime execution
[signal 0xb code=0x1 addr=0xa0 pc=0x473050]
